about.arc-body: Arc is the professional IDE for IBM Planning Analytics / TM1, with smart search, templates, blueprints, and a lightweight install. It gives developers a modern environment that makes development faster, more precise, and more enjoyable.
about.arc-impact: Developers work faster, with greater precision and unified coding practices.
about.arc-plus-body: Arc+ brings DevOps to IBM Planning Analytics / TM1 — automated testing, impact analysis, and deployment pipelines in one platform. Teams can build, test, and deploy with the same rigour as any modern software project.
about.arc-plus-impact: TM1 teams adopted modern software practices without leaving their ecosystem.
about.arc-plus-problem: TM1 teams were ready for DevOps — but had no platform for automated testing, impact analysis, and structured deployment.
about.arc-problem: TM1 developers deserved a professional IDE — not a basic editor that slowed them down.
about.atmosphere-body: Atmosphere connects IBM Planning Analytics / TM1 to the cloud with speed, security, and simplicity. With 34+ pre-built connectors, real-time data capture, and an intuitive admin portal — teams focus on planning, not plumbing.
about.atmosphere-impact: Integration became seamless, secure, and focused on outcomes rather than plumbing.
about.atmosphere-problem: Integrating TM1 with cloud data sources required complex middleware, long delivery cycles, and ongoing maintenance.
about.canvas-body: Canvas is a developer framework for building custom web frontends via the TM1 REST API. Cubewise partnered with Apliqo to evolve it into Apliqo UX — a no-code platform for rich web applications on top of IBM Planning Analytics.
about.canvas-impact: From code to no-code — TM1 became accessible to a broader audience.
about.canvas-problem: Customers needed rich, branded web interfaces on top of TM1 — but building them required deep developer expertise and significant effort.
about.cta-body: As IBM Planning Analytics / TM1 evolves, so will the Cubewise Platform. Talk to our team to find the right components for yours.
about.cta-heading: Innovation isn't a destination — it's a way of life
about.services-body: Beyond our software, Cubewise delivers implementation, consulting, and training services — grounded in decades of IBM Planning Analytics / TM1 expertise. Our consultants work alongside your team to accelerate rollouts, upskill users, and deliver complex projects with confidence.
about.hero-body: We build software that helps IBM Planning Analytics / TM1 teams work faster, smarter, and with more confidence — informed by deep expertise and close collaboration with the people who use it every day.
about.hero-heading: Built by TM1 experts,for TM1 teams
about.journey-heading: Solving real life challenges for IBM Planning Analytics teams.
about.mission-body-1: IBM Planning Analytics / TM1 is one of the most powerful planning platforms in the world — built for calculation speed, deep Excel integration, and data volumes that other platforms struggle to match. Cubewise was founded by experts who understood that power deeply, and who set out to make it even more accessible, productive, and professional for every team that relies on it.
about.mission-body-2: We listened to developers, administrators, and finance teams — and built software that amplifies what they can do with IBM Planning Analytics / TM1. The result is a platform of six specialised software, each one grounded in real-world experience and designed to work together seamlessly.
about.mission-body-3: a living library of TI code templates, best practices, and toolkits distilled from years of project work across industries. Bedrock reduced TI coding effort by up to 50% and established the standards on which everything else was built.
about.mission-quote: "Cubewise didn't set out to build software. We set out to solve problems."
about.powerconnect-body: PowerConnect brings IBM Planning Analytics data directly into Microsoft Power BI — with seamless synchronisation of data, metadata, and security. Executives and analysts can build live, interactive dashboards without manual exports.
about.powerconnect-impact: Executives gained real-time visibility into planning data with scheduled refreshes.
about.powerconnect-problem: Business intelligence teams wanted TM1 data in Power BI — but there was no reliable, secure way to connect the two platforms.
about.pulse-body: Pulse gives administrators complete visibility and control over their IBM Planning Analytics environment. Real-time monitoring, alerting, migration tooling, and full audit trails — in one place.
about.pulse-impact: Support calls dropped. User locks resolved proactively. Migrations de-risked.
about.pulse-problem: Administrators had limited visibility inside their TM1 environments — making monitoring, auditing, and migration unnecessarily risky.
about.slice-body: Slice is the modern Excel add-in for IBM Planning Analytics, with Active Forms, writeback, cross-drill, dynamic columns, and direct REST API connectivity. It honours Excel familiarity and adds the capabilities teams have always needed.
about.slice-impact: Smooth transition from Perspectives, with richer reporting and built-in auditing.
about.slice-problem: Finance teams needed a modern Excel experience for IBM Planning Analytics — with hierarchies, cloud connectivity, and REST API support.
about.tm1py-body: TM1py bridges IBM Planning Analytics / TM1 and Python, unlocking integrations with Salesforce, SAP, Google Sheets, and more. It opens the door to machine learning, forecasting, and anomaly detection on planning data.
about.tm1py-impact: TM1 became programmable, flexible, and future-ready.
about.tm1py-problem: Python was transforming data science and automation — but TM1 had no way to participate in that ecosystem.

arc-customers.hero-heading: What TM1 developers say about Arc
arc-customers.testimonial-1-quote: "Arc is quite simply a game changer for TM1 development which I cannot recommend highly enough. The efficiency it brings to the development cycle is undeniable, especially with the integration to our TM1 Cloud instances. As a developer, I cannot over-state just how quickly key features that have been missing for decades such as the VBA style debugging, autofills and rule section folding become essential. As system owner, I am fully aware of how much of an accelerant Arc is for our developers. Do yourself a favour, get a trial copy and don't look back!"
arc-customers.testimonial-1-role: Financial Data Analytics Director, Informa — UK
arc-customers.testimonial-2-quote: "I have been a TM1 developer for 18 years now. I've always loved TI and have managed to get around the timely nuances it brought. But with Arc, TI has been incredibly enhanced and improved. Being able to see multiple server stats, cube views, client security, rules, chores and of course TI processes all in one tool, just saves me so so much time. The formatting of code makes it easier to read, the improved find and replace, the detail to burst out data source and params, debugging and of course my fav the snippets. In short Arc is an incredible tool that takes TM1 administration and development to a new level and saves critical time for admins."
arc-customers.testimonial-2-role: TM1 Developer, Finance Development Team, News UK
arc-customers.testimonial-3-quote: "At Deutsche Bahn one of the biggest advantages of Arc is the possibility to easily switch between TM1 instances and migrate processes as we maintain over 70 TM1 instances on multiple servers. The other great advantage is Arc expansions which allow you to extend its capabilities yourself. On a daily basis I admire the little things that make life easier like syntax highlighting or tabs or a copy/paste that is actually working."
arc-customers.testimonial-3-role: Senior TM1 Consultant, Deutsche Bahn — Germany
arc-customers.testimonial-4-quote: "At Synlab, we have come to Arc from Cubewise after an extensive length of time using Notepad++. Having to copy/paste all four tabs back and forth to benefit the Search and Replace function was a bit tedious. We are more than delighted by the special attentions paid to details in Arc: components filtering, colour coding, search and replace, indentation, auto-complete, code snippets… Arc is really a must for the developers, and comes at a cheap price for so much added productivity."
arc-customers.testimonial-4-role: Group Financial and System Analyst, Synlab — Germany
arc-customers.testimonial-5-quote: "I am an independent TM1 Developer and I needed to access multiple environments for 5–10 different clients regularly, each with multiple servers and instances. With Arc I was able to access multiple environments easily and reproduce my coding across TIs and Rules between all these environments in a very consistent and efficient manner. There were occasions where we experienced downtime on cloud services due to IBM's Authentication issues, and Arc was still able to access the environments — enabling me to continue working."
arc-customers.testimonial-5-role: Senior TM1/PA Developer, EML Business Limited — UK
arc-customers.testimonial-6-quote: "I am paying for Arc out of my own pocket. Why? Because it's worth that much to me in productivity savings. The ability to use templates and saved code blocks, autocomplete, breakpoints, debugging… all the things that developers have been pining for years are here. But it goes far beyond the basics — the Tools node has some wonderful utilities as well. I'd encourage any TM1 developer to get a trial copy and see for themselves."
arc-customers.testimonial-6-role: TM1 Channel & TM1 Forum Legend — Australia
arc-customers.testimonial-7-quote: "Cubewise Arc is a real game changer as with it, you really get a true professional IDE for TM1. Our internal TM1 developer community had definitively adopted it and if, by bad luck, the Arc server is down, I'm informed in an instant. IBM is trying to catch up with Planning Analytics Workspace modelling but there's still a long way to cover."
arc-customers.testimonial-7-role: Business Analytics Software Engineering Expert — France
arc-customers.testimonial-8-quote: "Arc has made my development life so much easier. It has a great interface and features you'd expect like code completion. I love how quickly and easily I can search and find objects and code in my models."
arc-customers.testimonial-8-role: Finance Systems Manager / TM1 Developer, Magellan Asset Management — Australia

arc-plus-features.compare-dimensions-bod: Find differences between two dimensions or hierarchies — even across different server instances. Quickly identify missing elements or elements with different parents.

arc-plus-features.migration-body: Select multiple TM1 objects and migrate them all at once using Pulse — directly from Arc. Pulse automatically finds all dependencies, reducing the risk of missing objects during a migration.
arc-plus-features.model-search-body: Access Pulse Model Search from within Arc. Search across your entire TM1 model — rules, processes, dimensions, and more — to find objects and references instantly.
arc-plus-features.object-usage-body: Track TM1 object usage — processes, rules, subsets, cube views, and data changes — by sending activity data to Pulse. Visualise it in Pulse Explorer with the built-in User Objects Usage dashboard.
arc-plus-features.sessions-body: Monitor your TM1 server instances from within Arc via the upgraded Sessions Page. See active connections, running threads, and server health without leaving your development environment.

open-source-optimuspy.hero-label: Free dimension order optimiser for IBM Planning Analytics
open-source-optimuspy.importance-body: In IBM Planning Analytics / TM1, the order of dimensions in a cube directly affects RAM consumption and query performance. The wrong order can result in significantly higher memory usage and slower calculations — but finding the right order manually across many cubes is impractical.
open-source-optimuspy.importance-heading: Dimension order matters more than you think
open-source-optimuspy.importance-li-1: Dimension order directly impacts RAM usage and query speed
open-source-optimuspy.importance-li-2: The optimal order differs for every cube based on its data distribution
open-source-optimuspy.importance-li-3: Manual analysis across a large TM1 server is time-consuming and error-prone
open-source-optimuspy.importance-li-4: OptimusPy automates the entire analysis and recommendation process
open-source-optimuspy.measure-body: OptimusPy tests permutations of dimension order for each cube, measuring actual RAM consumption and query performance for each combination. It then identifies and reports the optimal ordering — backed by real measurements, not guesswork.
open-source-optimuspy.measure-li-1: Tests dimension order permutations against your live TM1 data
open-source-optimuspy.measure-li-2: Measures RAM usage and query execution time for each combination
open-source-optimuspy.measure-li-3: Identifies the optimal order per cube automatically
open-source-optimuspy.measure-li-4: Generates a clear report of recommended changes

open-source-rushti.fault-tolerant-body: Long-running load cycles shouldn't fail silently. RushTI includes checkpoint and resume capabilities so that a failed process doesn't force a full restart, and self-optimisation continually improves scheduling based on past run times.
open-source-rushti.fault-tolerant-li-1: Resume from the last successful checkpoint after a failure
open-source-rushti.fault-tolerant-li-2: Detailed logging for every process execution
open-source-rushti.fault-tolerant-li-3: Self-optimises scheduling order based on historical runtimes
open-source-rushti.fault-tolerant-li-4: Configurable retry logic for transient failures

open-source-rushti.parallel-body: TurboIntegrator processes typically run one at a time. RushTI removes that constraint, executing multiple processes simultaneously over a single TM1 connection — dramatically reducing total runtime for complex load cycles.
open-source-rushti.parallel-li-1: Execute many TI processes concurrently with one connection
open-source-rushti.parallel-li-2: Reduce load cycle runtimes by running independent processes in parallel
open-source-rushti.parallel-li-3: Define execution order with simple task files — no code required
open-source-rushti.parallel-li-4: Works with any IBM Planning Analytics / TM1 instance
open-source-rushti.scheduling-body: Not all processes can run at the same time. RushTI uses a directed acyclic graph (DAG) to model dependencies between tasks, ensuring processes only run once their prerequisites are complete — in the correct order, every time.
open-source-rushti.scheduling-li-2: DAG-based scheduling guarantees correct execution order
open-source-rushti.scheduling-li-3: Maximises parallelism while respecting data dependencies
open-source-rushti.scheduling-li-4: Visual dependency graphs for debugging and documentation

pulse-customer-aveva.about-body: Aveva is the world leading engineering software provider to the plant, power, and marine industries. Their Cognos TM1 model plays a critical role in financial planning, forecasting, and reporting for the business. As the company grows, demand for better quality and more timely information continues to increase.
pulse-customer-aveva.challenge-body: Aveva develops new models and enhances existing models in a development and UAT environment to reduce downtime risk in production. However, migrating tested models to production created significant challenges: knowing exactly which model components — dimensions, processes, cubes — were new versus already existing in production was difficult to determine manually, increasing the risk of missed or broken objects during deployment.
pulse-customer-aveva.cta-body: See how Pulse's migration and monitoring features can transform your TM1 operations.
pulse-customer-aveva.hero-heading: How Aveva transformed their TM1 model migration process
pulse-customer-aveva.hero-quote: "Pulse has given us great confidence knowing that everything necessary will have been migrated."
pulse-customer-aveva.results-li-1: Full confidence that every required object is included in each migration package
pulse-customer-aveva.results-li-2: Immediate alerts when TM1 servers go offline, enabling rapid resolution
pulse-customer-aveva.results-li-3: Change tracking with rollback provides a safety net for any production issues
pulse-customer-aveva.results-li-4: System diagrams help the team understand and enhance complex data flows
pulse-customer-aveva.results-li-5: Segregation of duties improves governance over the deployment process
pulse-customer-aveva.solution-body-1: Using Pulse's migration functionality, Aveva can automatically see all dependencies associated with any model object. Migration packages can be created in advance and deployed when ready, with Pulse handling the dependency resolution automatically.
pulse-customer-aveva.solution-body-2: Pulse also enabled separation of duties in the migration process: one administrator creates the package while another imports it after review — reducing risk and improving governance. Additional Pulse features brought further value:
pulse-customer-aveva.solution-li-1-body: immediate notification if TM1 servers go offline, enabling rapid diagnosis
pulse-customer-aveva.solution-li-2-body: full audit trail of changes to rules and processes, with rollback capability
pulse-customer-aveva.solution-li-3-body: visualise data flows between cubes and external sources
pulse-customer-aveva.solution-li-4-body: disconnect users causing blockages without impacting others
pulse-customer-norgine.about-body: Norgine is a leading European specialist pharmaceutical company with over 110 years of expertise and a presence in all major European markets. They use TM1 for financial planning, with users accessing data through multiple methods: TM1 Workflow, Perspectives directly in cube, Perspectives workbooks, and TM1 Web.
pulse-customer-norgine.challenge1-body: Norgine's financial planning process requires users to access TM1 through a specific Workflow system. Once users have write access, they can input data via Workflow, directly in Perspectives, through workbooks, or via TM1 Web. Without a unified monitoring tool, the team had no visibility into which access paths users were taking — making it impossible to identify performance issues or support users effectively.
pulse-customer-norgine.challenge1-headin: Challenge 1: Monitoring Users Across Multiple Access Methods
pulse-customer-norgine.challenge2-body: TM1 requires adequate disk space and memory to operate reliably — when disk space runs out, TM1 fails. Administrators were having to manually check multiple places (task manager, Windows Explorer) to monitor these critical resources, with no centralised view or automated alerting.
pulse-customer-norgine.challenge2-headin: Challenge 2: Monitoring Disk Space and Memory

pulse-customer-norgine.cta-heading: Get full visibility into your TM1 environment
pulse-customer-norgine.hero-heading: How Norgine gained full visibility into TM1 usage across all access methods
pulse-customer-norgine.hero-quote: "Pulse has standard functionality which gives reports showing a great amount of detail as to the actions of users in TM1 workbooks and also when using TM1 Web."
pulse-customer-norgine.results-li-1: Full visibility into user activity across all TM1 access methods — Workflow, Perspectives, TM1 Web
pulse-customer-norgine.results-li-2: Performance analysis identified workbooks over 30GB that should have been databases — enabling architecture improvements
pulse-customer-norgine.results-li-3: Cube usage data revealed unmet user needs and enabled targeted training programmes
pulse-customer-norgine.results-li-4: Centralised resource monitoring replaced manual checks, reducing administrative overhead
pulse-customer-norgine.results-li-5: Reduced user downtime through proactive identification and resolution of performance issues
pulse-customer-norgine.solution-body-1: Pulse provided detailed reporting on user actions across all TM1 access methods — Workflow, Perspectives, workbooks, and TM1 Web — in a single view. Performance analysis capabilities allowed the team to understand whether slowness was a genuine system issue or unrealistic user expectations.
pulse-customer-norgine.solution-body-2: Working with Cubewise, Norgine's specific need for TM1 Workflow user tracking was enabled — a feature that didn't previously exist in Pulse and was subsequently added to the product release.
pulse-customer-norgine.solution-body-3: Pulse's central monitoring dashboard brought disk space and memory visibility into one place, replacing manual checks across multiple system tools.
pulse-customer-pattonair.about-body: Pattonair is the leading global aerospace and defence supply chain service provider. Their TM1 model is core to business processes, accessed throughout the globe and required to be available near to 24/7. The model connects to hundreds of data sources, with loading processes required to run across multiple TM1 servers — daily and in some cases multiple times per day on demand.
pulse-customer-pattonair.challenge-body: With TM1 models accessed globally around the clock, Pattonair faced persistent challenges with user locks — and had no effective way to monitor users or be alerted when locks occurred. The scale and complexity of their environment meant performance issues were difficult to diagnose, and tracking which users were actively using the system versus working offline was impossible without the right tools.
pulse-customer-pattonair.cta-body: See how Pulse can help your team monitor and manage your TM1 environment.
pulse-customer-pattonair.hero-heading: How Pattonair reduced TM1 user locks and gained 24/7 visibility
pulse-customer-pattonair.hero-quote: "Pulse has helped us to significantly reduce user locks and allowed us to be notified via email if any user is locked in TM1 for more than 5 minutes."
pulse-customer-pattonair.results-li-1: Significantly reduced user locks through targeted performance enhancements informed by Pulse monitoring
pulse-customer-pattonair.results-li-2: Identified the most frequent users and those still working offline — enabling targeted training and knowledge sharing across the finance function
pulse-customer-pattonair.results-li-3: Used Pulse to monitor the success of new application deployments by tracking usage frequency
pulse-customer-pattonair.results-li-4: Confident that technical documentation for any model can be produced on demand through Pulse
pulse-customer-pattonair.results-li-5: Ability to monitor and track changes across production and development environments simultaneously
pulse-customer-pattonair.results-li-6: Can roll back any mistake without restoring from backup or taking servers offline
pulse-customer-pattonair.solution-body: Pattonair implemented Pulse to gain full visibility into their TM1 environment. Pulse's alert system enabled email notifications whenever a user was locked in TM1 for more than 5 minutes — allowing the team to act on issues before they became business problems. The monitoring capability also gave insight into Perspectives report usage, enabling the team to plan migrations to a more centralised and standardised reporting solution.
pulse-customer-scc.about-body: SCC is Europe's largest independent technology solutions company, providing European businesses and public sector organisations with IT infrastructure, cloud, and managed services. Their TM1 model is core to the business and downtime needs to be kept to an absolute minimum — especially during working hours. The model connects to multiple data sources, meaning any stop and restart of services risks disrupting data connectivity.
pulse-customer-scc.challenge-body: When migrating manually-created packages from development to production, SCC had to schedule downtime — a significant business disruption. There was also a high risk that objects could be missed during manual migrations, requiring the process to be repeated. With a model core to business operations, any period of unavailability had direct impact on users and data flows.
pulse-customer-scc.cta-body: Eliminate migration downtime and dependency risk with Pulse.
pulse-customer-scc.hero-heading: How SCC migrates TM1 packages live with zero downtime
pulse-customer-scc.hero-quote: "Pulse gives us as developers the option to migrate packages live without the need to bring down services."
pulse-customer-scc.results-li-1: Zero scheduled downtime for migrations — the system remains available 24/7
pulse-customer-scc.results-li-2: Automatic dependency detection removes the risk of missing objects during migration
pulse-customer-scc.results-li-3: Only changed objects are migrated, protecting existing configurations in the target environment
pulse-customer-scc.results-li-4: Time-consuming manual package creation eliminated, freeing developers for higher-value work
pulse-customer-scc.results-li-5: Faster handover of completed models to users without waiting for maintenance windows
pulse-customer-scc.solution-body-1: Pulse's migration functionality transformed SCC's deployment process. Rather than scheduling maintenance windows, developers can now migrate packages live while services remain running. Pulse automatically scans the model and attaches all dependencies to a migration package — so if you select a cube, Pulse automatically includes the dimensions and rules that build it, eliminating the risk of missing objects.
pulse-customer-scc.solution-body-2: When an object already exists in the target environment, Pulse only migrates the changes and differences — ensuring nothing that already exists is affected unnecessarily.
pulse-customer-stockland.about-body: Stockland is the largest diversified property group in Australia. With a large TM1 implementation and multiple internal and external developers working in parallel across different release cycles, managing change control and performance in production is a significant operational challenge.
pulse-customer-stockland.challenge1-body: Any changes to production systems must be tested, signed off, and approved before deployment. With multiple developers working in parallel and different release cycles, tracking what had changed in production — and by whom — was extremely difficult. When production issues arose after deployment, identifying the root cause was a time-consuming manual process.
pulse-customer-stockland.challenge2-body: When TM1 users reported system slowness, identifying the root cause was complex — issues could stem from model design, user behaviour, recent deployments, or concurrency locks. Native TM1 administration tools lacked the granularity needed to diagnose recurring performance issues efficiently.
pulse-customer-stockland.cta-body: Change tracking, rollback, and performance analytics — all in Pulse.
pulse-customer-stockland.cta-heading: Take control of your production environment
pulse-customer-stockland.example1-body: After a series of releases, Pulse alerts indicated users couldn't log into a TM1 instance. Using change tracking, the admin team discovered that an external contractor had incorrectly updated a process in production — the wrong version of a TurboIntegrator script had been deployed due to human error. The team could see exactly which lines were changed, rolled back to the correct version, and resolved the issue quickly with minimal user impact.
pulse-customer-stockland.example2-body: Using Pulse's user analytics, the admin team identified a recurring long-running thread that was causing wait times for users. They identified concurrent activities occurring simultaneously and worked with users to resolve the contention — eliminating a performance bottleneck that had been difficult to pinpoint with standard TM1 tools.
pulse-customer-stockland.hero-heading: How Stockland tracks changes and resolves performance issues across a large TM1 implementation
pulse-customer-stockland.hero-quote: "With Pulse, we are able to track all changes in the various environments and verify the correct changes have been made in production."
pulse-customer-stockland.results-li-1: Full audit trail of all changes across development and production environments
pulse-customer-stockland.results-li-2: Rapid identification and rollback of incorrect production deployments with minimal user impact
pulse-customer-stockland.results-li-3: Recurring performance bottlenecks identified and resolved using user analytics
pulse-customer-stockland.results-li-4: Confidence that correct versions have been deployed to production at all times
pulse-customer-stockland.results-li-5: Ability to manage multiple parallel development streams without losing track of production state
pulse-customer-stockland.solution-body-1: Pulse's change tracking logs every change made to TM1 objects — by administrators, users, or automated processes — recording who made the change, when, and which specific lines of rules or TurboIntegrator scripts were modified. Combined with rollback functionality, this gives the Stockland team the ability to restore any previous version quickly.
pulse-customer-stockland.solution-body-2: Pulse's user analytics feature allows the team to analyse system behaviour from a performance perspective, identifying long-running operations, concurrent activities, and the users involved — enabling targeted resolution rather than guesswork.
pulse-customers.aveva-quote: "Pulse has given us great confidence knowing that everything necessary will have been migrated."

slice-features.active-forms-body: Three ways to create reports from Slice: Slice, Snapshot, and Active Forms — the same options you know from Perspectives.
slice-features.annotations-body: Add comments to any cell in a Slice or Active Form via the right-click Annotations window. Attach context and notes directly to your data.
slice-features.buttons-body: Similar to Action Buttons in PA for Excel — trigger TM1 processes directly from your Excel reports without any scripting or developer involvement.
slice-features.cell-audit-body: The Cell Audit button helps you troubleshoot errors by showing exactly what's happening with a cell's value — great for diagnosing unexpected results in complex reports.
slice-features.cross-drill-body: Drill to another dimension on rows — for example, from Account to Region — for deeper analysis beyond simple expand/collapse.
slice-features.cta-body: Modern TM1 reporting for Excel users — no migration, no retraining.
slice-features.cube-viewer-body: Full support for hierarchies, MDX and native views, public and private views, search, snapshot/slice/active forms, and more. Powered by Arc for TM1.
slice-features.dbs-send-body: Trigger DBS functions only when required. Values that haven't been sent yet are displayed with an asterisk (*) so you always know what's pending.
slice-features.drillthrough-body: Drill through to a cube view or an external data source from any cell in a cube view, Slice, or Active Form. Explore underlying data without leaving your report.
slice-features.dynamic-columns-body: Slice Active Forms support dynamic columns. Insert an Active Form with dynamic columns and expand or collapse consolidations on columns — just like rows.
slice-features.group-new-desc: Capabilities that go beyond what Perspectives offered — built for modern TM1 reporting.
slice-features.group-traditional-desc: Everything you know from Perspectives and PA for Excel, fully supported in Slice.
slice-features.hero-body: Slice supports all traditional TM1 features — Excel functions, action buttons, sandboxes, and more — while adding powerful new capabilities not available in Perspectives.
slice-features.hero-heading: Traditional TM1 features, reimagined for Excel
slice-features.hierarchies-body: The DBHW function works like DBRW but supports hierarchies. Build Active Forms with full hierarchy support — something not possible in Perspectives.
slice-features.look-and-feel-body: The Excel ribbon includes all the traditional TM1 features you already know, plus innovative new ones. Designed to feel immediately familiar to anyone coming from Perspectives or PA for Excel.
slice-features.mdx-forms-body: A new report type driven by a single MDX query. MDX Forms give advanced users precise control over what data is retrieved and how it is presented in Excel.
slice-features.paste-range-body: Copy values from a range of cells and paste them into cells with DBRW functions without removing the functions. Bulk data entry made simple.
slice-features.remove-functions-body: Share reports with colleagues without TM1 access. Press Remove TM1 Functions and Slice replaces all formulas with static values — for the current worksheet or the whole workbook.
slice-features.report-burster-body: Burst Slices or Active Forms into a single or multiple files driven by a subset or MDX query. Automate the distribution of personalised reports across a large group of users.
slice-features.rollup-body: User Defined Consolidations are supported in the Arc cube viewer. Create consolidations calculated on the fly from the subset editor or directly from the cube view via the right-click menu.
slice-features.sandboxes-body: Create, merge, and reset sandboxes directly from the Excel ribbon. Work with what-if scenarios without affecting live data.
slice-features.subset-editor-body: All Architect features plus a search box to quickly find elements and a new way to visualise and edit MDX queries. Powered by Arc for TM1.
slice-features.tm1-functions-body: Fully backwards compatible with Perspectives and PA for Excel. All Excel TM1 functions are supported — DBRW, DBSW, SUBNM, and more — so your existing reports work without changes.
slice-features.trace-rules-body: Understand exactly how a cell value is calculated based on TM1 cube rules. Trace Rules makes it easy to debug unexpected values directly in Excel.
slice-features.virtual-consolidation-bod: Add additional calculations to a worksheet by selecting multiple elements or entering an MDX set expression — without modifying the TM1 model.
slice-features.virtual-expression-body: Add on-the-fly MDX-based calculations — AVG, MIN, MAX, STDDEV, and more — to an Excel worksheet without touching the TM1 model.
